title: The Zeal of Christ in Preaching
subtitle: How Christ called fishermen, not the wise, to found His Church

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the world fall away.
Between heaven and earth no joining can hold:
the heavenly, spiritual and light, draws upward;
the earthly, heavy and weighed down, presses below.
Let go of the weight. Be quiet, and come.
gospel: Three times the disciples were called.
First, they came only to faith,
to gain some knowledge and nearness of Jesus.
Then they followed Him in heart,
yet still returning to their own;
but they began to hear His teaching.
The third time they came to persevere with Him,
Jesus says to them: Come, follow me;
the eye is hidden away, and they follow Jesus.
One disciple says: Let me first go and bury my father.
He did not say, Leave me to be with my father,
but, Release me for the space of one hour.
And what did the Lord answer?
Even in a single hour you can perish.
behold: Consider, then, and behold the Lord Jesus
in the calling of His disciples and His life among them:
how lovingly He calls them,
making Himself to them gentle, familiar,
kind, and ready to serve;
drawing them within and without,
leading them to His Mother's house,
and going freely to their homes.
He taught them and instructed them,
and had a special care for them,
as a mother is wont to care for her only son.
For it is said that blessed Peter related
that when in some place He slept among them,
rising in the night He covered them again,
because most tenderly He loved them;
for He knew what He would make of them.
Behold also the obedience of those He called:
prompt, for at once and instantly they showed it;
whole, for they left all things;
right, for they followed the Lord Jesus.
The first perfect, the second more perfect, the third most perfect of all.
consider: Consider also by whom, and of what kind, the Church began.
The Lord did not will to choose the wise,
nor the powerful, nor the noble of this world,
lest the faith of the Gospel be ascribed
to their wisdom, their power, or their nobility.
Willing to break pride,
He chose the unlettered and the despised of the world,
and made these the teachers,
and sent them out to preach.
O blessed fishermen!
whom the Lord, among so many teachers of the Law and scribes,
among so many wise men of the age,
chose first for the office of divine preaching.
Humble in the world, even in their very trade,
but lofty in faith, devoted in service of mind;
lowborn to the world, but noble to Christ;
not written in the roll of an earthly senate,
but written in the roll of the senate in heaven;
despised of earth, but most welcome to heaven;
poor to the world, but rich to God.
Let the noble, the powerful, the wise of this world attend to this,
and know that the lowborn and the unlearned are set before them,
and from this be confounded and blush,
and come to their senses from their pride.
By the example of these our princes and leaders,
let us too leave all things, and follow Jesus,
in whom we have all things.
prayer: who as a diligent physician went about seeking the sick,
and healed the infirmities of bodies and of souls,
heal me, I pray, within and without,
draw me after You, that I may follow You,
passing over from vices to virtues,
Lead me out of the pit of misery and the mire of dregs,
and direct my steps into the way of salvation,
that I may advance in Your commandments,
and hasten to the blessed homeland,
and come there without hindrance. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Humiles quidem in seculo, etiam in ipso artis officio, sed præcelsos fide, ac devotos mentis obsequio; ignobiles seculo, sed nobiles Christo; despectos terræ, sed acceptissimos cælo; pauperes mundo, sed divites Deo.
latinJewel.english: Humble indeed in the world, even in their very trade, yet lofty in faith and devoted in the service of mind; lowborn to the world, but noble to Christ; despised of earth, but most welcome to heaven; poor to the world, but rich to God.
